full_response:
Step 1 :  What “natural units’’ means in 8.033  
Throughout the course we very often set  

 c = 1 (“geometrised’’ or “relativity’’ natural units).

Nothing else (ħ, G, …) is set to 1.  
With c = 1

• Velocities become pure numbers, measured in units of the speed of light.  
• Time and length acquire the same units.  If a natural–unit time t̃ is given the same
symbol t as a length, its numerical value is expressed in metres.  
To recover ordinary SI seconds we must divide by c, because

 t̃ (in metres) = c t_SI ⟹ t_SI = t̃ / c.

Step 2 :  Convert the velocity  

A velocity “5’’ in natural units is

 v = 5 c.

Insert the SI value of c:

 v_SI = 5 × 2.997 924 58 ×10^8 m s⁻¹
     ≈ 1.498 962 29 ×10^9 m s⁻¹.

Step 3 :  Convert the time  

A time “3’’ in natural units is 3 m in the geometrised system.  To get seconds

 t_SI = 3 m / c
     = 3 / (2.997 924 58 ×10^8) s
     ≈ 1.000 692 3 ×10^-8 s
     ≈ 10.0 ns. 

(That is the time light needs to travel 3 m.)

\boxed{\;v = 1.50 \times 10^{9}\ \text{m/s}, \qquad t = 1.0 \times 10^{-8}\ \text{s}\;}

Confidence: 34%
